title: APOTHECARY PRODUCTS Glass Mortar and Pestle Set, 4 Oz White Bowl for Medicine Mixing, Grinding, and Crushing Pills, Ideal for Pharmacy, Lab, and Home Use, Durable Medical-Grade Glass Mixe
description:
About this item
4 OUNCE CAPACITY: The 4 oz mortar and pestle set provides ample space for grinding and mixing, perfect for herbs, spices, and small batches of medication
CLEAR GLASS CONSTRUCTION: Made from durable clear glass, this mortar and pestle allows easy visibility of contents while ensuring long-lasting use
INCLUDES: Comes with both the mortar and pestle, offering a complete solution for grinding, crushing, and mixing ingredients or medications
BUILT TO LAST: Designed for durability, this high-quality mortar and pestle set is built to withstand repeated use, ensuring reliable performance over time
HEALTHY LIVING: Apothecary Products provides practical solutions to enhance wellness, supporting healthier lifestyles and improving health outcomes with every use
reviews_5s:
I use this to crush medications to administer them through a G-tube. It's very comfortable to hold and works well. The surface is very smooth and I'm able to get all the medication out of it without any difficulty. I use a small paintbrush to wipe the fine powder into the container I'm using to administer it. I have no complaints!It’s the perfect addition to my food display and works like a charm. I love the glass look and the quality of it! Would definitely recommend!Good for crushing pills. I have a few mortars/pill crushers this one (and the stainless one) so far are the best. The twist/mechanical/stone frustrated me because some of the pills will stuck in the nook/crannies and so hard to take em out.Right size and has a pour spout. Came on time and very pleasedwonderful size, lovely to showcase, heavy duty and made well.This is the perfect size for me.I recently purchased this pharmaceutical mortar and pestle for my home apothecary needs, and it has quickly become an indispensable tool in my kitchen. From the first use, it’s clear that this is a premium product designed for serious work.

Whether I’m compounding small batches of herbs or preparing specialized pharmaceutical mixtures, this mortar and pestle delivers consistent results every time. The ergonomic design allows for comfortable use, and the pestle fits perfectly in hand, giving you complete control over the grinding process. The size of the mortar is also perfect not too large, but spacious enough to handle a variety of tasks. It’s especially great for crushing pills or preparing fine powders, ensuring every particle is thoroughly ground.Bought it for grinding silver chloride (wear your gloves kids).
The interior is smooth & polished, so if one is going to do any serious grinding, I'd suggest sanding the interior & pestle with carbide sandpaper to give it a matte finish.Bon rapport qualité prix. Et comme broyeur de pilules il est beaucoup plus pratiques que les broyeurs électriques.Clean looking, and effective.
reviews_1s: this mortar and pestle worked great for grinding powders, until it broke on me today. there are tiny bubbles in the glass that are points of stress in the structure of the walls. apparently i applied too much pressure to these stress points and in an effort to release that stress, part of the wall popped off. i super glued the pieces back into place as best I could, and the same thing happened on a different spot of the bowl. it’s definitely not safe to use anymore. i only had this mortar and pestle for two months before this happened, so it wasn’t from extreme use or anything.The round bulb at the end of the pestle is a failure. I'm crushing pills with it, and it's very difficult to break up the pill—it keeps skittering away when you press. Once you do manage to get the pill started, the bottom of the mortar is flat, which does not work at all with the round pestle. You have almost no surface contact to work with to pulverize the pill. The ovoid shape you see on many pestles is not for looks—it's what makes the whole system work. There's also a slight bump on the bottom of the mortar, which makes grinding even harder.
